description: Browse and discover public channels and rooms available on the platform

actors.name: User
actors.description: Authenticated user browsing or searching the channel directory

actors.name: Administrator
actors.description: Manages which channels are visible in the directory and configures visibility settings

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: Returns paginated channel listings and enforces visibility permissions

general: Only rooms marked as public (open) are listed in the channel directory; private groups are excluded
Users with the view-c-room permission can see all public channels; users without it see only channels they are eligible to join
Directory listings are paginated; callers must supply offset and count to retrieve subsequent pages
The filter parameter performs a case-insensitive partial match against channel names and descriptions
Channels can be sorted by name, member count, or creation date; default sort is by name ascending
Federated channels from remote servers may appear in the directory if cross-server federation is enabled
The directory does not expose private or direct message rooms
A user who is already a member of a channel can still view it in the directory
The total count of matching channels is returned alongside each paginated response for UI rendering

directory_listed.result: A paginated list of public channels matching the filter and sort criteria is returned

channel_searched.result: Directory returns only channels whose names or descriptions match the filter string

given: user requests details for a specific channel by id or name
channel is public or user has view-c-room permission
channel_detail_retrieved.result: Full channel detail including description, member count, and type is returned

given: user is not authenticated or has been explicitly blocked from viewing channels
permission_denied.result: Request is rejected and an authorization error is returned

given: user applies a filter that matches no channels
no_results.result: An empty list is returned with a total count of zero

given: user requests details for a specific channel id or name that does not exist or is not public

errors.message: You do not have permission to browse the channel directory.
The requested channel was not found or is not publicly accessible.

events.name: channel_directory.listed
events.description: Fires when a user retrieves the paginated channel directory

events.name: channel_directory.searched
events.description: Fires when a user applies a text filter to the channel directory

events.name: channel_directory.detail_viewed
events.description: Fires when a user views the detail of a specific channel from the directory

related.reason: Channels listed in the directory are created and managed by channel management
View permissions determine which channels are visible to which users
Platform-wide search may surface channel directory results
Joining a channel from the directory updates the user's channel membership

goals.description: Return paginated, permission-filtered listings of public channels to help users discover and join relevant rooms

success_metrics.measurement: Private channels returned in directory results / total directory results
Time to return first page of directory results

human_checkpoints: before changing directory visibility settings that affect all users

invariants: only public channels appear in the directory
direct message rooms are never listed
pagination offset and count are required for all listing requests

acceptance_tests.given: workspace has both public and private channels

capabilities.description: Return filtered, sorted, paginated public channel listings with total count

tradeoffs.reason: private channels must never appear in the directory even if it limits the number of joinable channels shown
